name: Challa
recipeInstructions:
1. In the bowl of a heavy-duty electric mixer (KitchenAid K5A2 model works best), combine water, salt, and sugar. Stir to dissolve the sugar.
2. Add yeast and stir to dissolve.
3. Stir in beaten eggs and oil.
4. Beat in 5 cups of the weighed-out flour to make a batter. Beat for 5 minutes or until the batter begins to pull away from the sides of the bowl.
5. Knead remaining flour by machine until smooth. Place dough on a clean countertop and cover with a large metal bowl. Allow dough to double in bulk.
6. Punch dough down and allow it to rise again.
7. Punch dough down again and divide into two halves. Cut each half into thirds. Roll 3 pieces into ropes about 16 inches long using hands.
8. Join one end of each rope and press together with the heel of your hand. Alternatively, braid the three ropes together and press the other three ends together with the heel of your hand.
9. Sprinkle a large baking sheet with cornmeal. Place braided dough on the sheet pan and allow to double in bulk again. Repeat process with remaining dough.
10. Beat egg yolks and 1 tablespoon water together for egg wash. Lightly brush both risen braided doughs. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.
11. Bake in a preheated 350°F oven for 50-60 minutes, until loaves are deep golden brown and sound hollow when tapped on the bottoms with your finger. Remove to cooling rack.
